target_audience.primary_persona: Enterprise Integration Developers - technical professionals who build and maintain data synchronization workflows between business applications like CRMs, ERPs, and financial systems
secondary_personas: Business Systems Administrators who manage integrations between enterprise applications
IT consultants implementing integration solutions for clients
pain_points: Complex data transformations between disparate business systems with different APIs
Need for scheduled and on-demand data synchronization
Managing connections and credentials across multiple systems
Converting between SOAP, REST/JSON, and other API formats
Maintaining consistent data across CRM, accounting, and other business systems
goals: Create reliable integration workflows between business applications
Transform data between different formats (XML, JSON, SOAP)
Build reusable integration components and transformers
Deploy integration solutions to production environments
target_audience.usage_context: Used in enterprise environments to design, test, and deploy integration solutions. Developers use the Eclipse-based IDE to create transaction flows, while the Tomcat web portal handles authentication and configuration management. Solutions are deployed as WAR files to production servers.

product_vision.one_liner: Enterprise data integration platform for creating synchronization workflows between business applications
product_vision.problem_statement: Businesses need to synchronize data between multiple applications (CRMs, accounting software, payment processors) but each system uses different APIs and data formats, making integration complex and error-prone
product_vision.value_proposition: InterWeave IDE provides a visual development environment with a unique decoupling architecture where all data is converted to an internal IW XML format before transformation, enabling clean separation between source and destination systems while supporting SOAP, REST, and other API types
success_metrics: Number of active integration workflows deployed
Time saved vs. manual integration development
User adoption and workflow creation rate
System uptime and integration success rates

existing_features: Multiple adapter types (SOAP, REST/JSON, hierarchical, SQL)
Web portal for user and company management
Template editor for graphical data mapping
known_gaps: Custom user authentication not working (demo@sample.com login fails)
Need for modern Java version support
Single-user access to test server (no concurrent sessions)
technical_debt: LoginServlet uses proprietary password hash format that differs from database schema
Java 8 bundled JRE may need updating
Test server performance is slow (CPU/RAM constraints)
Windows batch scripts are primary (Linux scripts less maintained)

alternatives: MuleSoft Anypoint Platform - Enterprise integration and API management
Microsoft Power Automate - Low-code workflow automation
Informatica PowerCenter - Data integration and ETL
differentiators: Unique internal IW XML format decoupling architecture separates source/destination concerns
15+ years of production use and stability
Visual flow editor with conditional branching support
Supports complex enterprise APIs (SOAP, REST) not just simple webhooks
On-premise deployment option for data-sensitive enterprises
competitive_context.market_position: Legacy enterprise integration tool positioned for organizations needing complex SOAP/REST API integrations, particularly those with Salesforce, QuickBooks, Creatio, and payment processor integration needs. Competes with enterprise iPaaS solutions but with on-premise deployment advantages.

technical: Built on Eclipse 3.1 - legacy platform limits modernization
resources: Small development team (inferred from training meeting - 3-4 people)
Product has been in maintenance mode for existing clients
dependencies: External APIs: Salesforce, QuickBooks, Creatio, Authorize.Net, Stripe
